name: First State Bank and Trust
description_short: Community bank operating in Minnesota and Wisconsin offering checking, savings, mortgages, trust services, and digital banking products with FDIC insurance.
description_long: First State Bank and Trust (FSBT) is a community bank serving customers in Minnesota and Wisconsin. The bank positions itself as a relationship-focused financial institution that emphasizes personalized customer service and local presence. Founded on community banking principles, FSBT operates multiple branch locations and maintains an FDIC-insured deposit base backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal and business checking accounts, savings products, eChecking accounts, mortgages, credit cards, trust services, and digital banking solutions. Their digital offerings include mobile banking with transaction viewing, fund transfers, bill pay, mobile check deposit, and account alerts. FSBT also provides specialized services such as cash management for businesses, estate planning guidance, and financial roadmap planning across personal and business segments. FSBT distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized service and staff familiarity with customers. Customer testimonials highlight multi-decade loyalty (one customer reports 34 years of banking with them), responsive problem-solving (including account compromise resolution), and support for both individuals and nonprofit organizations. The bank offers convenience features like free check replacements for account changes and streamlined digital banking alongside traditional branch banking. As a regional community bank, FSBT serves a specific geographic footprint in MN and WI rather than operating nationally. The bank's strength lies in relationship banking and local decision-making rather than the technology infrastructure or product breadth of larger national institutions. Customers should verify that branch locations and product offerings meet their specific needs, and review current rates and terms as these would be competitive factors not detailed on the marketing-focused website content.
services: Mobile banking app with balance viewing and transfers
Mortgage lending and home purchase guidance
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Mobile banking app with check deposit, bill pay, fund transfers, and account alerts
Personalized customer service with staff familiarity (demonstrated by long-term customer loyalty)
Multiple financial roadmap planning tools for personal, business, estate, and family scenarios
Free replacement checks provided when account changes required (per customer testimonial)
Trust services and estate planning guidance available
Support for both individual and nonprofit organization banking needs
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Minnesota and Wisconsin only
No mention of competitive rates, APYs, or fee structures on website
No information about business loan products or commercial lending capabilities
Marketing-heavy website content lacks specific product details and terms
No disclosure of minimum balance requirements or account maintenance fees
best_for: Minnesota and Wisconsin residents seeking relationship-based community banking
Long-term customers valuing personal service and staff familiarity over digital-only banking
Individuals and families needing estate planning and trust services
Small business owners and nonprofit organizations in their service areas
diagnosis: First State Bank and Trust is best for customers in Minnesota and Wisconsin seeking relationship-driven community banking with personalized service, trust services, and estate planning. The primary caveat is limited geographic availability and lack of published rate/fee information on their website, requiring direct inquiry for specific product terms and competitiveness.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
